mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rate levels compress equity valuations by increasing the discount rate applied to future cash flows, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC > -0.03 in any 2-year window, or positive returns occur in >50% of months when FEDFUNDS is above median.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.5%, the labor market becomes sufficiently tight that wage inflation pressures emerge, prompting the Fed to tighten policy and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE < 4.5%, 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances, or Rank IC is not significantly negative at 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal robust consumer demand and income growth, driving corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and PAYEMS rise month-over-month, 20-day returns are positive in less than 55% of cases, or interaction term in regression is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation environment (CPIAUCSL year-over-year below 2.5%), rising nonfarm payrolls is positive for equities because it signals real economic strength without triggering monetary tightening.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL y/y < 2.5%, rising PAYEMS leads to negative 20-day returns in >50% of observations, or Rank IC is not positive at 10% significance.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales (RSAFS) directly signals increased consumer spending, which is positively correlated with near-term corporate earnings and equity prices over the following week.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS level changes with 5-day forward returns is below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production (INDPRO) exceeding 0.5% signals accelerating manufacturing activity and supply-side strength, which leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: When INDPRO m/m > 0.5%, 60-day forward returns are negative in >50% of cases, or the average return is not significantly greater than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is low (accommodative monetary policy) and housing starts (HOUST) are rising, the combination of cheap credit and expanding construction activity strongly boosts economic momentum and equity prices over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of low FEDFUNDS and rising HOUST does not produce a positive average 20-day return, or the interaction is not statistically significant in a linear regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a high unemployment regime (UNRATE above 6%), positive GDP growth indicates that the economy is recovering from a recession, which strongly supports equity prices as earnings rebound from depressed levels.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 6%, quarterly GDP growth does not lead to positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in >60% of observations, or the conditional correlation is negative.
